meta_title: For Freelancers: deployment workflow to Solve stuck with basic apps (Intermediate Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to deployment workflow and overcome stuck with basic apps. A Intermediate-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.
h2_structure: The Problem: stuck with basic apps

content.seo_title: Freelancer Deployment Workflow: Scale Beyond Basic Apps
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with basic app deployments. Master a professional deployment workflow using Antigravity and Claude Code to deliver high-quality client projects.
content.answer_block: Transform your deployment bottleneck by implementing an automated, infrastructure-as-code workflow. By integrating Antigravity for environment provisioning and Claude Code for intelligent CLI orchestration, you eliminate manual configuration errors, ensure production parity, and drastically reduce your time-to-market, allowing you to ship professional-grade SaaS applications to your clients with total confidence.
content.intro:
You know the feeling. The prototype runs perfectly on your local machine, but the moment you push to production, the wheels fall off. You’re trapped in a cycle of manual server configuration and 'it works on my machine' excuses, watching your billable hours evaporate while your client loses patience.

It’s time to stop hacking your deployments together and start engineering them. This guide replaces the amateur 'trial-and-error' approach with a hardened, repeatable deployment pipeline designed for freelancers who need to ship fast, stay stable, and maintain a reputation for excellence.

steps.title: Automated Environment Provisioning
steps.description: Standardize your infrastructure so every client project starts from a clean, production-ready state.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a Terraform configuration that provisions a scalable VPC, RDS instance, and an ECS cluster with best-practice security group rules for a Node.js SaaS application.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Initialize infrastructure project structure using Antigravity output'

steps.title: CI/CD Pipeline Orchestration
steps.description: Create a robust deployment pipeline that handles testing, building, and deploying automatically.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a GitHub Actions workflow file that runs unit tests, builds a Docker image, pushes it to ECR, and triggers a rolling update on ECS.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Integrate CI/CD YAML into project root and verify pipeline secrets'

steps.title: Zero-Downtime Deployment Strategy
steps.description: Implement blue-green deployment logic to ensure your client's users never see a 500 error during updates.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure an AWS Application Load Balancer with target group health checks for blue-green deployment patterns.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Configure ALB routing policies for zero-downtime releases'

steps.title: Automated Observability Integration
steps.description: Inject logging and monitoring before the first user hits the production URL.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Add CloudWatch logging and Sentry error tracking initialization to the container entrypoint script.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Implement logging middleware and configure Sentry DSN integration'

steps.title: Production Smoke Test Suite
steps.description: Run automated post-deployment checks to verify the system health instantly.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a Playwright script that performs a smoke test on the production login and dashboard routes.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Add smoke test suite to CI/CD pipeline post-deployment step'

pro_tips: Always use ephemeral environments for feature branches to allow clients to preview changes without impacting production.
Tag your infrastructure resources with client IDs and project names to simplify automated cost reporting for billing.
Implement a 'circuit breaker' pattern in your deployment script that automatically rolls back if health checks fail twice.
Use secret management services like AWS Secrets Manager rather than environment variables to prevent credential leakage in logs.
Keep your infrastructure code in the same repository as your application code to ensure version alignment.
content.next_steps: Once your pipeline is live, set up a recurring monthly maintenance task to rotate secrets and update container base images to prevent security drift.
content.conversion_block: Stop settling for basic apps and start building like a professional agency. Leverage these tools to scale your freelance business and dominate the market. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
